summary: Replaced the hand-written assembly implementation of Dolphin ARQ with a matching C implementation and public header. The PR removed asm/dolphin/ar/arq.s, added src/dolphin/ar/arq.c and src/dolphin/ar/arq.h, and updated obj_files.mk to build arq.c.o instead of arq.s.o. The implementation models ARQ high/low request queues, pending requests, callbacks, chunked low-priority DMA transfers, DMA interrupt servicing, initialization, posting requests under disabled interrupts, and ARQGetChunkSize. The PR body says it was based on Prime decomp. There were no review comments; one collaborator review approved it.
what_changed: Added src/dolphin/ar/arq.c with C implementations for __ARQPopTaskQueueHi, __ARQServiceQueueLo, __ARQCallbackHack, __ARQInterruptServiceRoutine, ARQInit, ARQPostRequest, and ARQGetChunkSize.
Added src/dolphin/ar/arq.h defining ARQ_CHUNK_SIZE_DEFAULT, ARQType, ARQPrio, ARQRequest, ARQCallback, and ARQ function prototypes.
Changed obj_files.mk so the ARQ object is built from $(BUILD_DIR)/src/dolphin/ar/arq.c.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/dolphin/ar/arq.s.o.
Converted the ARQ sbss globals from assembly symbols into static C state: high/low queue heads and tails, pending requests, callbacks, chunk size, and volatile init flag.
Implemented high-priority queue popping as a helper, replacing duplicated high-priority start-DMA logic that appeared in the original assembly interrupt/post paths.
Implemented low-priority servicing as chunked DMA work using __ARQChunkSize and setting the low callback only when the remaining request length fits in the current transfer.

Implemented ARQPostRequest to fill the ARQRequest fields, install a no-op callback when callback is NULL, enqueue by ARQ priority, kick off DMA if no request is pending, and restore interrupts afterward.
smart_moves: Factored the repeated high-priority DMA scheduling sequence into __ARQPopTaskQueueHi, which keeps the C source compact while preserving the observed behavior.
Kept the original internal Dolphin-style double-underscore names for ARQ internals, making the source easy to compare against the deleted assembly symbols.
Used explicit ARQType and ARQPrio enums plus an ARQRequest struct in the new header to document field offsets that were only implicit in the assembly.
Preserved the MRAM-to-ARAM versus ARAM-to-MRAM argument swap around ARStartDMA, which is central to matching the original control flow.
Preserved the NULL callback behavior by assigning __ARQCallbackHack rather than branching around callback storage later.
Used OSDisableInterrupts and OSRestoreInterrupts around queue mutation in ARQPostRequest, matching the interrupt-sensitive nature of ARQ queue state.
Declared __ARQ_init_flag as static volatile bool, consistent with the assembly's small-data init guard while representing it idiomatically in C.
Kept ARQ_CHUNK_SIZE_DEFAULT as a named header constant with the observed value 4096 instead of hardcoding 0x1000 in ARQInit.
decomp_lessons: When replacing Dolphin library assembly, first recover the request struct field layout from offsets; here next, owner, type, priority, source, dest, length, and callback map to the assembly stores and loads.
For queue-driven interrupt code, model both queue-head/tail state and pending/current-operation state explicitly; ARQ uses separate high and low queues plus pending high/low slots.
A small helper can improve C readability and still match if it corresponds to a repeated assembly block or produces compatible call structure.

Chunked transfer code may intentionally update length/source/dest by a fixed chunk size even when the final transfer length is shorter or equal; preserve the original arithmetic instead of normalizing it unless matching proves otherwise.
External decomp sources can be useful matching references, but the PR only states 'Based on prime decomp'; future reuse should still validate against Melee's target assembly.
After migrating an assembly object to C, update obj_files.mk in the same PR so the build switches from asm/*.s.o to src/*.c.o.
Header additions should carry both public API declarations and internal prototypes when other compilation units or matching constraints need stable names.
naming_conventions: Internal ARQ functions and state use Dolphin-style double underscores, e.g. __ARQServiceQueueLo, __ARQInterruptServiceRoutine, __ARQRequestQueueHi, and __ARQChunkSize.
Public ARQ APIs use ARQ-prefixed PascalCase names, e.g. ARQInit, ARQPostRequest, and ARQGetChunkSize.
Enums use ARQ_TYPE_* for transfer direction and ARQ_PRIORITY_* for queue priority.
The default chunk size macro is named ARQ_CHUNK_SIZE_DEFAULT.
The init flag preserves the original mixed-case symbol style as __ARQ_init_flag rather than forcing a new naming scheme.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted sbss assembly globals into file-scope static variables in the same logical order: high queue, high tail, low queue, low tail, pending high, pending low, callback high, callback low, chunk size, init flag.
Matched the ARStartDMA call argument order by branching on ARQ_TYPE_MRAM_TO_ARAM: MRAM-to-ARAM passes source then dest, otherwise passes dest then source.
Kept the low-priority queue service behavior where only the final chunk installs __ARQCallbackLo, while intermediate chunks continue advancing the request by __ARQChunkSize.
Preserved the interrupt callback priority: service high callback first, otherwise low callback, then pop high queue, then service low queue only if no high request is pending.
Used a switch on ARQPrio in ARQPostRequest to reproduce the assembly's priority dispatch for low and high queues.
Maintained a no-op __ARQCallbackHack function so NULL callbacks still become callable function pointers, matching the assembly's fallback branch.
Replaced the assembly object in obj_files.mk with the C object, which is the build-system step required to prove the new C is matching.

review_feedback.note: No issue comments or review comments were present in the PR slice, so there is no detailed reviewer rationale to extract.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify src/dolphin/ar/arq.c or src/dolphin/ar/arq.h for fixes to ARQ matching or API completeness.
Search for uses of ARQReset, __ARQInitTempQueue, and __ARQPushTempQueue because this header declares them but this PR slice does not show implementations.
Compare this ARQ implementation against other Dolphin SDK or Prime decomp ARQ sources to understand which naming and struct-layout choices were imported.
Check whether later matching work adjusted __ARQServiceQueueLo's chunk length arithmetic or callback timing.
Search obj_files.mk migrations from asm/dolphin/* to src/dolphin/* for reusable patterns in replacing Dolphin library assembly.
